18	    - [Compare with reference implementation](#compare-with-reference-implementation)
19	    - [gpt-j with triton backend](#gpt-j-with-triton-backend)
20	
21	## Introduction
22	
23	This document describes the step to run the GPT-J model on FasterTransformer.
24	GPT-J was developed by EleutherAI and trained on The Pile, a 825GB dataset from curated sources (e.g. Wikipedia, arXiv, GitHub, StackExchange, PubMed, ...).
25	With 6 billion parameters, GPT-J is one of the largest GPT-like publicly released models as of 2021.
26	
27	Optimization in GPT-j are similar to optimization in GPT, describing in the [gpt_guide.md](gpt_guide.md#optimization).
28	
29	* Constructor of GPT-j
30	
31	| Classification |             Name             |     Data Type      |                                                                                                            Description                                                                                                            |
32	| :------------: | :--------------------------: | :----------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
33	|      [0]       |        max_batch_size        |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
34	|      [1]       |         max_seq_len          |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
35	|      [2]       |        max_input_len         |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
36	|      [3]       |          beam_width          |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
37	|      [4]       |           head_num           |       size_t       |                                                                                                Head number for model configuration                                                                                                |
38	|      [5]       |        size_per_head         |       size_t       |                                                                                               Size per head for model configuration                                                                                               |
39	|      [6]       |          inter_size          |       size_t       |                                                                     The inter size of feed forward network. It is often set to 4 * head_num * size_per_head.                                                                      |
40	|      [7]       |          num_layer           |       size_t       |                                                                                       Number of transformer layers for model configuration                                                                                        |
41	|      [8]       |          vocab_size          |       size_t       |                                                                                              Vocabulary size for model configuration                                                                                              |
42	|      [9]       |     rotary_embeeding_dim     |       size_t       |                                                                          Rotary embedding dimension of rotary position embedding for model configuration                                                                          |
43	|      [10]      |           start_id           |        int         |                                                                                                      Start id for vocabulary                                                                                                      |
44	|      [11]      |            end_id            |        int         |                                                                                                       End id for vocabulary                                                                                                       |
45	|      [12]      |   prompt_learning_start_id   |        int         |                                                                                         The start id of virtual token in p/prompt-tuning                                                                                          |
46	|      [13]      |     prompt_learning_type     | PromptLearningType |                                   The type of prompt learning when we load the prompt embedding in constructor. FT supports `no_prompt`, `soft_prompt`, `prefix_prompt`, `p_prompt_tuning` now                                    |
47	|      [14]      |  beam_search_diversity_rate  |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
48	|      [15]      |            top_k             |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
49	|      [16]      |            top_p             |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
50	|      [17]      |         random_seed          | unsigned long long |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
51	|      [18]      |         temperature          |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
52	|      [19]      |         len_penalty          |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
53	|      [20]      |      repetition_penalty      |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
54	|      [21]      |         tensor_para          |     NcclParam      |                                                                   Tensor Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                    |
55	|      [22]      |        pipeline_para         |     NcclParam      |                                                                  Pipeline Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                   |
56	|      [23]      |            stream            |    cudaStream_t    |                                                                                                            CUDA stream                                                                                                            |
57	|      [24]      |        cublas_wrapper        |  cublasMMWrapper*  |                                                                  Pointer of cuBLAS wrapper, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/cublasMMWrapper.h`                                                                  |
58	|      [25]      |          allocator           |    IAllocator*     |                                                                    Pointer of memory allocator, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/allocator.h`                                                                    |
59	|      [26]      | is_free_buffer_after_forward |        bool        | If setting to be `true`, FasterTransformer will allocate buffer before forward, and free buffer after forward. When the allocator is based on memory pool, setting to `true` may help reducing the memory usage during inference. |
60	|      [27]      |       cuda_device_prop       |  cudaDeviceProp*   |                                                           Pointer of CUDA device properties, which is used to get the properties of hardware like size of shared memory                                                           |
61	|      [28]      |    custom_all_reduce_comm    | AbstractCustomComm |                                                Custom all reduction communication for custom all reduction in model parallelism. It is only supported in 8-way tensor parallelism                                                 |
62	|      [29]      |   enable_custom_all_reduce   |        int         |                                                                                           Flag of enabling custom all reduction or not                                                                                            |
63	
64	* Input of GPT-j
65	
66	|             Name              |            Tensor/Parameter Shape             | Location |       Data Type        |                                                               Description                                                               |
67	| :---------------------------: | :-------------------------------------------: | :------: | :--------------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
68	|           input_ids           |        [batch_size, max_input_length]         |   GPU    |          int           |                                                         The input ids (context)                                                         |
69	|         input_lengths         |                 [batch_size]                  |   GPU    |          int           |                                                        The lengths of input ids                                                         |
70	| prompt_learning_task_name_ids |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                                            **Optional**. Task name ids for prompt learning.                                             |
71	|        output_seq_len         |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |        uint32_t        |                        The largest number of tokens you hope for results. Note that it contains the input length                        |
72	|           start_id            |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                             **Optional**. If FT receives this input, FT will replace default start id by it                             |
73	|            end_id             |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                              **Optional**. If FT receives this input, FT will replace default end id by it                              |
74	|        stop_words_list        |      [batch_size, 2, stop_words_length]       |   GPU    |          int           |                                       **Optional**. FT would not generate the tokens in the list.                                       |
75	|        bad_words_list         |       [batch_size, 2, bad_words_length]       |   GPU    |          int           |                                       **Optional**. The words in the list will never be sampled.                                        |
76	|         runtime_top_k         |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |          uint          |                                              **Optional**. top_k value for top k sampling                                               |
77	|         runtime_top_p         |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                                              **Optional**. top_p value for top p sampling                                               |
78	|  beam_search_diversity_rate   |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                **Optional**. A hyper hyper-parameter for [simple diverse decoding](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1611.08562.pdf)                |
79	|          temperature          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                              **Optional**. Temperature applied to logits for both beam search and sampling                              |
80	|          len_penalty          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                                   **Optional**. Length penalty applied to logits for only beam search                                   |
81	|      repetition_penalty       |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                          **Optional**. Repetition penalty applied to logits for both beam search and sampling. Exclusive with presence_penalty.                          |
82	|       presence_penalty        |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                                                  **Optional**. Presence penalty - additive type of repetition penalty - applied to logits for both beam search and sampling. Exclusive with repetition_penalty.                                                  |
83	|           min_length          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |          int           |                                                  **Optional**. Minimum number of tokens to generate                                                  |
84	|          random_seed          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    | unsigned long long int |                                  **Optional**. Random seed to initialize the random table in sampling.                                  |
85	|    request_prompt_lengths     |                 [batch_size],                 |   GPU    |          int           |     **Optional**. Length of prefix soft prompt embedding. This describes how many tokens of soft prompt embedding in each sentence.     |
86	|   request_prompt_embedding    | [batch_size, max_prompt_length, hidden_units] |   GPU    |         float          |                 **Optional**. Prefix soft prompt embedding. FT will concat them with results of embedding lookup kernel                 |
87	|      request_prompt_type      |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                  **Optional**. Prompt type of request. This is necessary when user pass the prompt embedding by input                   |
88	|          memory_len           |                      [1]                      |   CPU    |         uint32         | **Optional**. The maximum time memory used in attention modules. Reduces the memory footprint but quality of generation might degrades. |
89	
90	* Output of GPT-j
91	
92	|       Name       |              Tensor/Parameter Shape              | Location | Data Type |                                    Description                                    |
93	| :--------------: | :----------------------------------------------: | :------: | :-------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
94	|    output_ids    |   [batch_size, beam_width, max_output_seq_len]   |   GPU    |    int    |            The output ids. It contains the input_ids and generated ids            |
95	| sequence_length  |             [batch_size, beam_width]             |   GPU    |    int    |                             The lengths of output ids                             |
96	| output_log_probs | [batch_size, beam_width, request_output_seq_len] |   GPU    |   float   | **Optional**. It records the log probability of logits at each step for sampling. |
97	|  cum_log_probs   |             [batch_size, beam_width]             |   GPU    |   float   |          **Optional**. Cumulative log probability of generated sentences          |
98	
99	The `beam_width` value is set by the output shape directly. When the `beam_width` of `output_ids` is larger than 1, FT will use beam search to generate tokens; otherwise, FT will use topk or topp sampling. When the inputs of beam search and sampling is invalid, like beam width 1, top k 0, top p 0.0, FT will run greedy search automatically.
100	
101	### Inference Options
102	
103	We provide the environment variables to tune for specific usage.
104	
105	|        Name        |             Description                         |              Default                         |              Values accepted                         |
106	| :----------------: | :----------------------------------------------: | :----------------------------------------------: | :----------------------------------------------: |
107	|  `FMHA_ENABLE`     |   enable the fused multi-head attention kernels (fp16 accumulation)   | disabled | `ON` = enable fmha, otherwise disabled |
108	|  `CONTEXT_ATTENTION_BMM1_HALF_ACCUM`     |   use fp16 accumulation for the qk gemm, and only make a difference to unfused multi-head attention kernels | fp32 accumulation | `ON` = fp32 accumulation, otherwise fp16 accumulation |
109	
110	### Supported features
111	
112	* Checkpoint converter
113	  * EleutherAI
114	  * Huggingface
115	* Data type
116	  * FP32
117	  * FP16
118	  * BF16
119	* Feature
120	  * Multi-GPU multi-node inference

# GPT-J

## Table Of Contents

- [GPT-J](#gpt-j)
  - [Table Of Contents](#table-of-contents)
  - [Introduction](#introduction)
    - [Inference Options](#inference-options)
    - [Supported features](#supported-features)
  - [Setup](#setup)
    - [Requirements](#requirements)
    - [Docker image](#docker-image)
    - [Build project](#build-project)
    - [Download the model](#download-the-model)
    - [Download tables](#download-tables)
    - [Run GPT-J](#run-gpt-j)
    - [Run GPTJ with prompts](#run-gptj-with-prompts)
    - [Compare with reference implementation](#compare-with-reference-implementation)
    - [gpt-j with triton backend](#gpt-j-with-triton-backend)

## Introduction

This document describes the step to run the GPT-J model on FasterTransformer.
GPT-J was developed by EleutherAI and trained on The Pile, a 825GB dataset from curated sources (e.g. Wikipedia, arXiv, GitHub, StackExchange, PubMed, ...).
With 6 billion parameters, GPT-J is one of the largest GPT-like publicly released models as of 2021.

Optimization in GPT-j are similar to optimization in GPT, describing in the [gpt_guide.md](gpt_guide.md#optimization).

* Constructor of GPT-j

| Classification |             Name             |     Data Type      |                                                                                                            Description                                                                                                            |
| :------------: | :--------------------------: | :----------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
|      [0]       |        max_batch_size        |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [1]       |         max_seq_len          |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [2]       |        max_input_len         |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [3]       |          beam_width          |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [4]       |           head_num           |       size_t       |                                                                                                Head number for model configuration                                                                                                |
|      [5]       |        size_per_head         |       size_t       |                                                                                               Size per head for model configuration                                                                                               |
|      [6]       |          inter_size          |       size_t       |                                                                     The inter size of feed forward network. It is often set to 4 * head_num * size_per_head.                                                                      |
|      [7]       |          num_layer           |       size_t       |                                                                                       Number of transformer layers for model configuration                                                                                        |
|      [8]       |          vocab_size          |       size_t       |                                                                                              Vocabulary size for model configuration                                                                                              |
|      [9]       |     rotary_embeeding_dim     |       size_t       |                                                                          Rotary embedding dimension of rotary position embedding for model configuration                                                                          |
|      [10]      |           start_id           |        int         |                                                                                                      Start id for vocabulary                                                                                                      |
|      [11]      |            end_id            |        int         |                                                                                                       End id for vocabulary                                                                                                       |
|      [12]      |   prompt_learning_start_id   |        int         |                                                                                         The start id of virtual token in p/prompt-tuning                                                                                          |
|      [13]      |     prompt_learning_type     | PromptLearningType |                                   The type of prompt learning when we load the prompt embedding in constructor. FT supports `no_prompt`, `soft_prompt`, `prefix_prompt`, `p_prompt_tuning` now                                    |
|      [14]      |  beam_search_diversity_rate  |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [15]      |            top_k             |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [16]      |            top_p             |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [17]      |         random_seed          | unsigned long long |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [18]      |         temperature          |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [19]      |         len_penalty          |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [20]      |      repetition_penalty      |       float        |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [21]      |         tensor_para          |     NcclParam      |                                                                   Tensor Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                    |
|      [22]      |        pipeline_para         |     NcclParam      |                                                                  Pipeline Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                   |
|      [23]      |            stream            |    cudaStream_t    |                                                                                                            CUDA stream                                                                                                            |
|      [24]      |        cublas_wrapper        |  cublasMMWrapper*  |                                                                  Pointer of cuBLAS wrapper, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/cublasMMWrapper.h`                                                                  |
|      [25]      |          allocator           |    IAllocator*     |                                                                    Pointer of memory allocator, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/allocator.h`                                                                    |
|      [26]      | is_free_buffer_after_forward |        bool        | If setting to be `true`, FasterTransformer will allocate buffer before forward, and free buffer after forward. When the allocator is based on memory pool, setting to `true` may help reducing the memory usage during inference. |
|      [27]      |       cuda_device_prop       |  cudaDeviceProp*   |                                                           Pointer of CUDA device properties, which is used to get the properties of hardware like size of shared memory                                                           |
|      [28]      |    custom_all_reduce_comm    | AbstractCustomComm |                                                Custom all reduction communication for custom all reduction in model parallelism. It is only supported in 8-way tensor parallelism                                                 |
|      [29]      |   enable_custom_all_reduce   |        int         |                                                                                           Flag of enabling custom all reduction or not                                                                                            |

* Input of GPT-j

|             Name              |            Tensor/Parameter Shape             | Location |       Data Type        |                                                               Description                                                               |
| :---------------------------: | :-------------------------------------------: | :------: | :--------------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
|           input_ids           |        [batch_size, max_input_length]         |   GPU    |          int           |                                                         The input ids (context)                                                         |
|         input_lengths         |                 [batch_size]                  |   GPU    |          int           |                                                        The lengths of input ids                                                         |
| prompt_learning_task_name_ids |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                                            **Optional**. Task name ids for prompt learning.                                             |
|        output_seq_len         |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |        uint32_t        |                        The largest number of tokens you hope for results. Note that it contains the input length                        |
|           start_id            |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                             **Optional**. If FT receives this input, FT will replace default start id by it                             |
|            end_id             |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                              **Optional**. If FT receives this input, FT will replace default end id by it                              |
|        stop_words_list        |      [batch_size, 2, stop_words_length]       |   GPU    |          int           |                                       **Optional**. FT would not generate the tokens in the list.                                       |
|        bad_words_list         |       [batch_size, 2, bad_words_length]       |   GPU    |          int           |                                       **Optional**. The words in the list will never be sampled.                                        |
|         runtime_top_k         |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |          uint          |                                              **Optional**. top_k value for top k sampling                                               |
|         runtime_top_p         |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                                              **Optional**. top_p value for top p sampling                                               |
|  beam_search_diversity_rate   |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                **Optional**. A hyper hyper-parameter for [simple diverse decoding](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1611.08562.pdf)                |
|          temperature          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                              **Optional**. Temperature applied to logits for both beam search and sampling                              |
|          len_penalty          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                                   **Optional**. Length penalty applied to logits for only beam search                                   |
|      repetition_penalty       |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                          **Optional**. Repetition penalty applied to logits for both beam search and sampling. Exclusive with presence_penalty.                          |
|       presence_penalty        |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |         float          |                                                  **Optional**. Presence penalty - additive type of repetition penalty - applied to logits for both beam search and sampling. Exclusive with repetition_penalty.                                                  |
|           min_length          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    |          int           |                                                  **Optional**. Minimum number of tokens to generate                                                  |
|          random_seed          |              [1] or [batch_size]              |   CPU    | unsigned long long int |                                  **Optional**. Random seed to initialize the random table in sampling.                                  |
|    request_prompt_lengths     |                 [batch_size],                 |   GPU    |          int           |     **Optional**. Length of prefix soft prompt embedding. This describes how many tokens of soft prompt embedding in each sentence.     |
|   request_prompt_embedding    | [batch_size, max_prompt_length, hidden_units] |   GPU    |         float          |                 **Optional**. Prefix soft prompt embedding. FT will concat them with results of embedding lookup kernel                 |
|      request_prompt_type      |                 [batch_size]                  |   CPU    |          int           |                  **Optional**. Prompt type of request. This is necessary when user pass the prompt embedding by input                   |
|          memory_len           |                      [1]                      |   CPU    |         uint32         | **Optional**. The maximum time memory used in attention modules. Reduces the memory footprint but quality of generation might degrades. |

* Output of GPT-j

|       Name       |              Tensor/Parameter Shape              | Location | Data Type |                                    Description                                    |
| :--------------: | :----------------------------------------------: | :------: | :-------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
|    output_ids    |   [batch_size, beam_width, max_output_seq_len]   |   GPU    |    int    |            The output ids. It contains the input_ids and generated ids            |
| sequence_length  |             [batch_size, beam_width]             |   GPU    |    int    |                             The lengths of output ids                             |
| output_log_probs | [batch_size, beam_width, request_output_seq_len] |   GPU    |   float   | **Optional**. It records the log probability of logits at each step for sampling. |
|  cum_log_probs   |             [batch_size, beam_width]             |   GPU    |   float   |          **Optional**. Cumulative log probability of generated sentences          |

The `beam_width` value is set by the output shape directly. When the `beam_width` of `output_ids` is larger than 1, FT will use beam search to generate tokens; otherwise, FT will use topk or topp sampling. When the inputs of beam search and sampling is invalid, like beam width 1, top k 0, top p 0.0, FT will run greedy search automatically.

### Inference Options

We provide the environment variables to tune for specific usage.

|        Name        |             Description                         |              Default                         |              Values accepted                         |
| :----------------: | :----------------------------------------------: | :----------------------------------------------: | :----------------------------------------------: |
|  `FMHA_ENABLE`     |   enable the fused multi-head attention kernels (fp16 accumulation)   | disabled | `ON` = enable fmha, otherwise disabled |
|  `CONTEXT_ATTENTION_BMM1_HALF_ACCUM`     |   use fp16 accumulation for the qk gemm, and only make a difference to unfused multi-head attention kernels | fp32 accumulation | `ON` = fp32 accumulation, otherwise fp16 accumulation |

### Supported features

* Checkpoint converter
  * EleutherAI
  * Huggingface
* Data type
  * FP32
  * FP16
  * BF16
* Feature
  * Multi-GPU multi-node inference

1	# FasterTransformer BART
2	
3	The FasterTransformer BART implements the huggingface BART model (https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/model_doc/bart).
4	
5	## Table Of Contents
6	
7	- [FasterTransformer BART](#fastertransformer-bart)
8	  - [Table Of Contents](#table-of-contents)
9	  - [Introduction](#introduction)
10	    - [Supported features](#supported-features)
11	    - [Optimization](#optimization)
12	  - [Setup](#setup)
13	    - [Requirements](#requirements)
14	    - [Build FasterTransformer](#build-fastertransformer)
15	      - [Prepare](#prepare)
16	      - [Build the project](#build-the-project)
17	  - [How to use](#how-to-use)
18	
19	## Introduction
20	
21	This document describes what FasterTransformer provides for the `BART` model, explaining the workflow and optimization. We also provide a guide to help users to run the `BART` model on FasterTransformer. Finally, we provide benchmark to demonstrate the speed of FasterTransformer on `BART`. 
22	
23	### Supported features
24	
25	* Checkpoint loading
26	  * Huggingface
27	* Data type
28	  * FP32
29	  * FP16
30	  * BF16
31	* Feature
32	  * Multi-GPU multi-node inference
33	  * Dynamic random seed
34	  * Stop tokens
35	  * Beam search and sampling are both supported
36	* Frameworks
37	  * PyTorch
38	
39	### Optimization
40	
41	1.	Kernel optimization: First, since the sequence length of query in `SelfAttention` and `CrossAttention` is always 1, we use customed fused multi-head attention kernel to optimize. Second, we fuse many small operations into one kernel. For example, `AddBiasResidualLayerNorm` combines the adding bias, adding residual of previous block and the computation of layer normalization into 1 kernel. Third, we optimize top k operation and sampling to accelerate the beam search and sampling. Finally, to prevent from recomputing the previous keys and values, we allocate a buffer to store them at each step. Although it takes some additional memory usage, we can save the cost of recomputing, allocating buffer at each step, and the cost of concatenation.
42	
43	## Setup
44	
45	The following section lists the requirements to use FasterTransformer.
46	
47	### Requirements
48	
49	- CMake >= 3.13 for PyTorch
50	- CUDA 11.0 or newer version
51	- NCCL 2.10 or newer version
52	- Python: Only verify on Python 3.
53	- PyTorch: Verify on 1.10.0, >= 1.5.0 should work.
54	
55	Recommend use nvcr image like `nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:22.09-py3`.

59	- [NVIDIA Pascal](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/pascal-gpu-architecture/) or [Volta](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/volta-gpu-architecture/) or [Turing](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/geforce/turing/) or [Ampere](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/nvidia-ampere-gpu-architecture/) based GPU 
60	
61	For more information about how to get started with NGC containers, see the following sections from the NVIDIA GPU Cloud Documentation and the Deep Learning Documentation:
62	
63	- [Getting Started Using NVIDIA GPU Cloud](https://docs.nvidia.com/ngc/ngc-getting-started-guide/index.html)
64	- [Accessing And Pulling From The NGC Container Registry](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/frameworks/user-guide/index.html#accessing_registry)
65	- [Running PyTorch](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/frameworks/pytorch-release-notes/index.html)
66	
67	For those unable to use the NGC container, to set up the required environment or create your own container, see the versioned [NVIDIA Container Support Matrix](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/frameworks/support-matrix/index.html).
68	
69	### Build FasterTransformer

77	    git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA/FasterTransformer.git

# FasterTransformer BART

The FasterTransformer BART implements the huggingface BART model (https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/model_doc/bart).

## Table Of Contents

- [FasterTransformer BART](#fastertransformer-bart)
  - [Table Of Contents](#table-of-contents)
  - [Introduction](#introduction)
    - [Supported features](#supported-features)
    - [Optimization](#optimization)
  - [Setup](#setup)
    - [Requirements](#requirements)
    - [Build FasterTransformer](#build-fastertransformer)
      - [Prepare](#prepare)
      - [Build the project](#build-the-project)
  - [How to use](#how-to-use)

## Introduction

This document describes what FasterTransformer provides for the `BART` model, explaining the workflow and optimization. We also provide a guide to help users to run the `BART` model on FasterTransformer. Finally, we provide benchmark to demonstrate the speed of FasterTransformer on `BART`. 

### Supported features

* Checkpoint loading
  * Huggingface
* Data type
  * FP32
  * FP16
  * BF16
* Feature
  * Multi-GPU multi-node inference
  * Dynamic random seed
  * Stop tokens
  * Beam search and sampling are both supported
* Frameworks
  * PyTorch

### Optimization

1.	Kernel optimization: First, since the sequence length of query in `SelfAttention` and `CrossAttention` is always 1, we use customed fused multi-head attention kernel to optimize. Second, we fuse many small operations into one kernel. For example, `AddBiasResidualLayerNorm` combines the adding bias, adding residual of previous block and the computation of layer normalization into 1 kernel. Third, we optimize top k operation and sampling to accelerate the beam search and sampling. Finally, to prevent from recomputing the previous keys and values, we allocate a buffer to store them at each step. Although it takes some additional memory usage, we can save the cost of recomputing, allocating buffer at each step, and the cost of concatenation.

## Setup

The following section lists the requirements to use FasterTransformer.

### Requirements

- CMake >= 3.13 for PyTorch
- CUDA 11.0 or newer version
- NCCL 2.10 or newer version
- Python: Only verify on Python 3.
- PyTorch: Verify on 1.10.0, >= 1.5.0 should work.

Recommend use nvcr image like `nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:22.09-py3`.

Ensure you have the following components:
- [NVIDIA Docker](https://github.com/NVIDIA/nvidia-docker) and NGC container are recommended
- [NVIDIA Pascal](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/pascal-gpu-architecture/) or [Volta](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/volta-gpu-architecture/) or [Turing](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/geforce/turing/) or [Ampere](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/nvidia-ampere-gpu-architecture/) based GPU 

For more information about how to get started with NGC containers, see the following sections from the NVIDIA GPU Cloud Documentation and the Deep Learning Documentation:

- [Getting Started Using NVIDIA GPU Cloud](https://docs.nvidia.com/ngc/ngc-getting-started-guide/index.html)
- [Accessing And Pulling From The NGC Container Registry](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/frameworks/user-guide/index.html#accessing_registry)
- [Running PyTorch](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/frameworks/pytorch-release-notes/index.html)

For those unable to use the NGC container, to set up the required environment or create your own container, see the versioned [NVIDIA Container Support Matrix](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/frameworks/support-matrix/index.html).

### Build FasterTransformer

#### Prepare

You can choose the pytorch version and python version you want. Here, we suggest image `nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:22.09-py3`, which contains the PyTorch 1.13.0 and python 3.8.

    ```bash
    nvidia-docker run -ti --shm-size 5g --rm nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:22.09-py3 bash
    git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA/FasterTransformer.git
    mkdir -p FasterTransformer/build
    cd FasterTransformer/build
    git submodule init && git submodule update
    ```

#### Build the project

* Note: the `xx` of `-DSM=xx` in following scripts means the compute capability of your GPU. The following table shows the compute capability of common GPUs.

|  GPU  | compute capacity |
| :---: | :--------------: |
|  P40  |        60        |
|  P4   |        61        |

29	      - [T5-small on V100-16GB](#t5-small-on-v100-16gb)
30	
31	## Introduction
32	
33	This document describes what FasterTransformer provides for the `T5` model, explaining the workflow and optimization. We also provide a guide to help users to run the `T5` model on FasterTransformer. Finally, we provide benchmark to demonstrate the speed of FasterTransformer on `T5`.
34	
35	### Supported features
36	
37	* Checkpoint converter
38	  * Huggingface
39	  * Megatron
40	  * NeMo Megatron
41	* Data type
42	  * FP32
43	  * FP16
44	  * BF16
45	* Feature
46	  * Multi-GPU multi-node inference
47	  * Dynamic random seed
48	  * Stop tokens
49	  * Beam search and sampling are both supported
50	  * Loading FP32 or FP16 weights
51	* Frameworks
52	  * PyTorch
53	  * Triton backend
54	
55	## Model architecture
56	
57	### Workflow
58	
59	The source codes are put in `src/fastertransformer/models/t5`.
60	
61	* Constructor of T5 Encoder
62	
63	| Classification |             Name             |     Data Type      |                                                                                                            Description                                                                                                            |
64	| :------------: | :--------------------------: | :----------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
65	|      [0]       |        max_batch_size        |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
66	|      [1]       |         max_seq_len          |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
67	|      [2]       |           head_num           |       size_t       |                                                                                                Head number for model configuration                                                                                                |
68	|      [3]       |        size_per_head         |       size_t       |                                                                                               Size per head for model configuration                                                                                               |
69	|      [4]       |          inter_size          |       size_t       |                                                                    The inter size of feed forward network. It is often set to 4 \* head_num \* size_per_head.                                                                     |
70	|      [5]       |           d_model            |       size_t       |                                                                                         The dimension of embedding of transformer input.                                                                                          |
71	|      [6]       |          num_layer           |       size_t       |                                                                                       Number of transformer layers for model configuration                                                                                        |
72	|      [7]       |  num_bucket_or_max_seq_len   |       size_t       |                                                              Number of bucket in relative position embedding, or max sequence length for absolute position embedding                                                              |
73	|      [8]       |         max_distance         |       size_t       |                                                                                           Max distance for relative position embedding                                                                                            |
74	|      [9]       |              sm              |        int         |                                                                                                    The compute capacity of GPU                                                                                                    |
75	|      [10]      |          q_scaling           |       float        |                                                                          It is used to scale the query before the batch multiplication of query and key                                                                           |
76	|      [11]      |            stream            |    cudaStream_t    |                                                                                                            CUDA stream                                                                                                            |
77	|      [12]      |        cublas_wrapper        |  cublasMMWrapper*  |                                                                  Pointer of cuBLAS wrapper, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/cublasMMWrapper.h`                                                                  |
78	|      [13]      |          allocator           |    IAllocator*     |                                                                    Pointer of memory allocator, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/allocator.h`                                                                    |
79	|      [14]      | is_free_buffer_after_forward |        bool        | If setting to be `true`, FasterTransformer will allocate buffer before forward, and free buffer after forward. When the allocator is based on memory pool, setting to `true` may help reducing the memory usage during inference. |
80	|      [15]      |        attention_type        |   AttentionType    |                                      Determine fusing the attention or not, remove padding or not, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/BaseAttentionLayer.h`                                      |
81	|      [16]      |            sparse            |        bool        |                                                                                            Is using sparsity. **Experimental feature**                                                                                            |
82	|      [17]      |       activation_type        |   ActivationType   |                                                         Determine the activation in FFN, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/FfnLayer.h`                                                          |
83	|      [18]      |        layernorm_type        |   LayerNormType    |                       Determine using pre-layernorm or post-layernorm, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/kernels/layernorm_kernels.h`. Note that only `pre_layernorm` is supported at the moment.                       |
84	|      [19]      |         tensor_para          |     NcclParam      |                                                                   Tensor Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                    |
85	|      [20]      |        pipeline_para         |     NcclParam      |                                                                  Pipeline Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                   |
86	|      [21]      |   prompt_learning_start_id   |        int         |                                                                                         The start id of virtual token in p/prompt-tuning                                                                                          |
87	|      [22]      |     prompt_learning_type     | PromptLearningType |                                   The type of prompt learning when we load the prompt embedding in constructor. FT supports `no_prompt`, `soft_prompt`, `prefix_prompt`, `p_prompt_tuning` now                                    |
88	|      [23]      |    custom_all_reduce_comm    | AbstractCustomComm |                                                Custom all reduction communication for custom all reduction in model parallelism. It is only supported in 8-way tensor parallelism                                                 |
89	|      [24]      |   enable_custom_all_reduce   |        int         |                                                                                           Flag of enabling custom all reduction or not                                                                                            |
90	
91	* Input of T5 Encoder
92	
93	|             Name              |            Tensor/Parameter Shape             | Location |   Data Type    |                                                           Description                                                           |
94	| :---------------------------: | :-------------------------------------------: | :------: | :------------: | :-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
95	|           input_ids           |             [batch_size, seq_len]             |   GPU    |      int       |                                                          The input ids                                                          |

    - [Running mt5](#running-mt5)
  - [Performance](#performance)
    - [End-to-end translation performance on PyTorch](#end-to-end-translation-performance-on-pytorch)
      - [T5-3B on A100-80GB](#t5-3b-on-a100-80gb)
      - [T5-base on A100-40GB](#t5-base-on-a100-40gb)
      - [T5-base on V100-16GB](#t5-base-on-v100-16gb)
      - [T5-small on V100-16GB](#t5-small-on-v100-16gb)

## Introduction

This document describes what FasterTransformer provides for the `T5` model, explaining the workflow and optimization. We also provide a guide to help users to run the `T5` model on FasterTransformer. Finally, we provide benchmark to demonstrate the speed of FasterTransformer on `T5`.

### Supported features

* Checkpoint converter
  * Huggingface
  * Megatron
  * NeMo Megatron
* Data type
  * FP32
  * FP16
  * BF16
* Feature
  * Multi-GPU multi-node inference
  * Dynamic random seed
  * Stop tokens
  * Beam search and sampling are both supported
  * Loading FP32 or FP16 weights
* Frameworks
  * PyTorch
  * Triton backend

## Model architecture

### Workflow

The source codes are put in `src/fastertransformer/models/t5`.

* Constructor of T5 Encoder

| Classification |             Name             |     Data Type      |                                                                                                            Description                                                                                                            |
| :------------: | :--------------------------: | :----------------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |
|      [0]       |        max_batch_size        |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [1]       |         max_seq_len          |       size_t       |                                                                                                   **Deprecated, move to input**                                                                                                   |
|      [2]       |           head_num           |       size_t       |                                                                                                Head number for model configuration                                                                                                |
|      [3]       |        size_per_head         |       size_t       |                                                                                               Size per head for model configuration                                                                                               |
|      [4]       |          inter_size          |       size_t       |                                                                    The inter size of feed forward network. It is often set to 4 \* head_num \* size_per_head.                                                                     |
|      [5]       |           d_model            |       size_t       |                                                                                         The dimension of embedding of transformer input.                                                                                          |
|      [6]       |          num_layer           |       size_t       |                                                                                       Number of transformer layers for model configuration                                                                                        |
|      [7]       |  num_bucket_or_max_seq_len   |       size_t       |                                                              Number of bucket in relative position embedding, or max sequence length for absolute position embedding                                                              |
|      [8]       |         max_distance         |       size_t       |                                                                                           Max distance for relative position embedding                                                                                            |
|      [9]       |              sm              |        int         |                                                                                                    The compute capacity of GPU                                                                                                    |
|      [10]      |          q_scaling           |       float        |                                                                          It is used to scale the query before the batch multiplication of query and key                                                                           |
|      [11]      |            stream            |    cudaStream_t    |                                                                                                            CUDA stream                                                                                                            |
|      [12]      |        cublas_wrapper        |  cublasMMWrapper*  |                                                                  Pointer of cuBLAS wrapper, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/cublasMMWrapper.h`                                                                  |
|      [13]      |          allocator           |    IAllocator*     |                                                                    Pointer of memory allocator, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/allocator.h`                                                                    |
|      [14]      | is_free_buffer_after_forward |        bool        | If setting to be `true`, FasterTransformer will allocate buffer before forward, and free buffer after forward. When the allocator is based on memory pool, setting to `true` may help reducing the memory usage during inference. |
|      [15]      |        attention_type        |   AttentionType    |                                      Determine fusing the attention or not, remove padding or not, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/BaseAttentionLayer.h`                                      |
|      [16]      |            sparse            |        bool        |                                                                                            Is using sparsity. **Experimental feature**                                                                                            |
|      [17]      |       activation_type        |   ActivationType   |                                                         Determine the activation in FFN, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/FfnLayer.h`                                                          |
|      [18]      |        layernorm_type        |   LayerNormType    |                       Determine using pre-layernorm or post-layernorm, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/kernels/layernorm_kernels.h`. Note that only `pre_layernorm` is supported at the moment.                       |
|      [19]      |         tensor_para          |     NcclParam      |                                                                   Tensor Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                    |
|      [20]      |        pipeline_para         |     NcclParam      |                                                                  Pipeline Parallel information, which is declared in `src/fastertransformer/utils/nccl_utils.h`                                                                   |
|      [21]      |   prompt_learning_start_id   |        int         |                                                                                         The start id of virtual token in p/prompt-tuning                                                                                          |
|      [22]      |     prompt_learning_type     | PromptLearningType |                                   The type of prompt learning when we load the prompt embedding in constructor. FT supports `no_prompt`, `soft_prompt`, `prefix_prompt`, `p_prompt_tuning` now                                    |
|      [23]      |    custom_all_reduce_comm    | AbstractCustomComm |                                                Custom all reduction communication for custom all reduction in model parallelism. It is only supported in 8-way tensor parallelism                                                 |
|      [24]      |   enable_custom_all_reduce   |        int         |                                                                                           Flag of enabling custom all reduction or not                                                                                            |

<result>Based on my analysis of the FasterTransformer codebase, here is a concise structured summary of the rotary position embedding implementation:

---

## 1. Functions That Apply Rotary Embedding

**Primary implementation:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1355-1568` - Core `apply_rotary_embedding` template functions for various vector types (float2, float4, uint32_t, uint2, uint4, __nv_bfloat162, bf16_4_t, bf16_8_t)

**Key functions:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1325-1329` - `rotary_embedding_coefficient` - computes cos/sin coefficients
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1331-1337` - `rotary_embedding_transform` - applies 2D rotation transform
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1355-1568` - Multiple `apply_rotary_embedding` overloads for different types

**Usage locations:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/unfused_attention_kernels.cu:1426-1465` - In `add_fusedQKV_bias_transpose_kernel`
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_template.hpp:1324-1385` - In masked MHA kernel

---

## 2. How `neox_rotary_style` Changes Rotation Math

**GPT-J Style (`neox_rotary_style == false`):**
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/unfused_attention_kernels.cu:1426-1428`
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_template.hpp:1324-1331`
- **Direct interleaved rotation** - rotates adjacent pairs of elements:
  ```cpp
  if (!neox_rotary_style) {
      mmha::apply_rotary_embedding(q, k, tidx, rotary_embedding_dim, dst_kv_seq_idx);
  }
  ```

**GPT-NeoX Style (`neox_rotary_style == true`):**
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/unfused_attention_kernels.cu:1429-1465`
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_template.hpp:1332-1385`
- **rotate_half (first half vs second half)** - more complex implementation:
  1. Stores Q/K to shared memory (`q_smem`, `k_smem`) at line 1432-1443 (template.hpp: 1335-1351)
  2. Transposes data via `vec_from_smem_transpose` at line 1450-1451 (template.hpp: 1358-1361)
  3. Applies rotary embedding on transposed data at line 1453 (template.hpp: 1363-1370)
  4. Writes back via `write_smem_transpose` at line 1455-1456 (template.hpp: 1366, 1372)
  5. Reads back from shared memory at line 1462-1463 (template.hpp: 1377-1382)

The key difference is the **transpose step** which reorganizes data so rotation happens between the first and second halves of the rotary dimension, rather than adjacent pairs.

---

## 3. Rotary Invocation in GPT-J/GPT-NeoX Decoder Self-Attention

**Layer class member variables:**
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:40-41`:
  ```cpp
  const size_t rotary_embedding_dim_;
  const bool   neox_rotary_style_;
  ```

**Passed to attention dispatch:**
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.cc:596-597`:
  ```cpp
  rotary_embedding_dim_,
  neox_rotary_style_,
  ```

**Dispatch function signature:**
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.cc:50-51` (in function `fusedQKV_masked_attention_dispatch`)
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:168-169` (declaration)

**Parameter struct:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention.h:90-91`:
  ```cpp
  int  rotary_embedding_dim = 0;
  bool neox_rotary_style    = false;
  ```

---

## 4. Constructor Signature Differences

**TensorParallelDecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:35-51** - Main constructor with rotary params:
```cpp
TensorParallelDecoderSelfAttentionLayer(size_t                              max_batch_size,
                                        size_t                              head_num,
                                        size_t                              size_per_head,
                                        size_t                              rotary_embedding_dim,    // 4th param
                                        bool                                neox_rotary_style,       // 5th param
                                        size_t                              d_model,
                                        float                               q_scaling,
                                        NcclParam                           tensor_para,
                                        cudaStream_t                        stream,
                                        cublasMMWrapper*                    cublas_wrapper,
                                        IAllocator*                         allocator,
                                        bool                                do_all_reduce,
                                        bool                                is_free_buffer_after_forward,
                                        bool                                is_sparse                = false,
                                        int                                 int8_mode                = 0,
                                        std::shared_ptr<AbstractCustomComm> custom_all_reduce_comm   = nullptr,
                                        int                                 enable_custom_all_reduce = 0);
```

**Alternative constructors** (lines 53-81, 83-97) that don't specify rotary parameters default to `rotary_embedding_dim=0` and `neox_rotary_style=false`.

**DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:72-78** - Base layer constructor:
```cpp
DecoderSelfAttentionLayer(size_t           max_batch_size,
                          size_t           head_num,
                          size_t           size_per_head,
                          size_t           local_head_num,
                          size_t           rotary_embedding_dim,  // 5th param
                          bool             neox_rotary_style,      // 6th param
                          size_t           d_model,
                          const float      q_scaling,
                          ...
```

---

This implementation aligns with the standard definitions: GPT-J uses interleaved rotary pairs, while GPT-NeoX uses the rotate_half approach (rotating first half against second half of the dimension).</result>

Based on my analysis of the FasterTransformer codebase, here is a concise structured summary of the rotary position embedding implementation:

---

## 1. Functions That Apply Rotary Embedding

**Primary implementation:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1355-1568` - Core `apply_rotary_embedding` template functions for various vector types (float2, float4, uint32_t, uint2, uint4, __nv_bfloat162, bf16_4_t, bf16_8_t)

**Key functions:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1325-1329` - `rotary_embedding_coefficient` - computes cos/sin coefficients
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1331-1337` - `rotary_embedding_transform` - applies 2D rotation transform
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_utils.h:1355-1568` - Multiple `apply_rotary_embedding` overloads for different types

**Usage locations:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/unfused_attention_kernels.cu:1426-1465` - In `add_fusedQKV_bias_transpose_kernel`
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_template.hpp:1324-1385` - In masked MHA kernel

---

## 2. How `neox_rotary_style` Changes Rotation Math

**GPT-J Style (`neox_rotary_style == false`):**
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/unfused_attention_kernels.cu:1426-1428`
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_template.hpp:1324-1331`
- **Direct interleaved rotation** - rotates adjacent pairs of elements:
  ```cpp
  if (!neox_rotary_style) {
      mmha::apply_rotary_embedding(q, k, tidx, rotary_embedding_dim, dst_kv_seq_idx);
  }
  ```

**GPT-NeoX Style (`neox_rotary_style == true`):**
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/unfused_attention_kernels.cu:1429-1465`
- Location: `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention/decoder_masked_multihead_attention_template.hpp:1332-1385`
- **rotate_half (first half vs second half)** - more complex implementation:
  1. Stores Q/K to shared memory (`q_smem`, `k_smem`) at line 1432-1443 (template.hpp: 1335-1351)
  2. Transposes data via `vec_from_smem_transpose` at line 1450-1451 (template.hpp: 1358-1361)
  3. Applies rotary embedding on transposed data at line 1453 (template.hpp: 1363-1370)
  4. Writes back via `write_smem_transpose` at line 1455-1456 (template.hpp: 1366, 1372)
  5. Reads back from shared memory at line 1462-1463 (template.hpp: 1377-1382)

The key difference is the **transpose step** which reorganizes data so rotation happens between the first and second halves of the rotary dimension, rather than adjacent pairs.

---

## 3. Rotary Invocation in GPT-J/GPT-NeoX Decoder Self-Attention

**Layer class member variables:**
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:40-41`:
  ```cpp
  const size_t rotary_embedding_dim_;
  const bool   neox_rotary_style_;
  ```

**Passed to attention dispatch:**
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.cc:596-597`:
  ```cpp
  rotary_embedding_dim_,
  neox_rotary_style_,
  ```

**Dispatch function signature:**
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.cc:50-51` (in function `fusedQKV_masked_attention_dispatch`)
- `src/fastertransformer/layers/attention_layers/DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:168-169` (declaration)

**Parameter struct:**
- `src/fastertransformer/kernels/decoder_masked_multihead_attention.h:90-91`:
  ```cpp
  int  rotary_embedding_dim = 0;
  bool neox_rotary_style    = false;
  ```

---

## 4. Constructor Signature Differences

**TensorParallelDecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:35-51** - Main constructor with rotary params:
```cpp
TensorParallelDecoderSelfAttentionLayer(size_t                              max_batch_size,
                                        size_t                              head_num,
                                        size_t                              size_per_head,
                                        size_t                              rotary_embedding_dim,    // 4th param
                                        bool                                neox_rotary_style,       // 5th param
                                        size_t                              d_model,
                                        float                               q_scaling,
                                        NcclParam                           tensor_para,
                                        cudaStream_t                        stream,
                                        cublasMMWrapper*                    cublas_wrapper,
                                        IAllocator*                         allocator,
                                        bool                                do_all_reduce,
                                        bool                                is_free_buffer_after_forward,
                                        bool                                is_sparse                = false,
                                        int                                 int8_mode                = 0,
                                        std::shared_ptr<AbstractCustomComm> custom_all_reduce_comm   = nullptr,
                                        int                                 enable_custom_all_reduce = 0);
```

**Alternative constructors** (lines 53-81, 83-97) that don't specify rotary parameters default to `rotary_embedding_dim=0` and `neox_rotary_style=false`.

**DecoderSelfAttentionLayer.h:72-78** - Base layer constructor:
```cpp
DecoderSelfAttentionLayer(size_t           max_batch_size,
                          size_t           head_num,
                          size_t           size_per_head,
                          size_t           local_head_num,
                          size_t           rotary_embedding_dim,  // 5th param
                          bool             neox_rotary_style,      // 6th param
                          size_t           d_model,
                          const float      q_scaling,
                          ...
```

---

This implementation aligns with the standard definitions: GPT-J uses interleaved rotary pairs, while GPT-NeoX uses the rotate_half approach (rotating first half against second half of the dimension).

For the standard GPT activation, looking at ParallelGpt — it uses GeluFFN typically. Actually it can be configured. Let me just say gelu (default).

Let me also note the architectural parallel vs serial:
- GPT, BART, T5: serial transformer (attention then FFN sequentially)
- GPT-J: parallel (attention & FFN computed simultaneously from same normed input, outputs summed)
- GPT-NeoX: configurable via use_gptj_residual (the 20B model uses parallel/GPT-J style)

This is a great distinguishing insight. Now let me write the lecture.

- GPT-J and GPT-NeoX: directly mirror ParallelGpt's two-phase (context + decoder) split, with GptJContextDecoder/GptJDecoder and GptNeoXContextDecoder/GptNeoXDecoder. The forward loop in GptJ.cc/GptNeoX.cc is nearly identical to ParallelGpt.cc.

- GPT family (GPT/GPT-J/GPT-NeoX) = decoder-only, use context+decoder two-phase split because the prompt IS the context.
- BART/T5 = encoder-decoder, the encoder handles the source sequence (the "context" equivalent), and *Decoding only runs the single-step generation loop.
